title: Representing Audio for Generation (Spectrograms)
concept: Audio is often converted into a spectrogram (a 2D image-like representation of frequency over time), letting image-generation techniques apply directly.

exercises.prompt: When you talk into a phone, what does your voice get turned into so a computer can handle it?

options.text: Nothing, it stays a sound wave forever

exercises.explain: Computers can't store sound waves directly — they turn audio into数据 (data), like numbers, that can be processed and later turned back into sound.

cards.title: Can You Draw a Sound?
cards.body: What if you could take a song and turn it into a picture — one that shows exactly how it changes over time? Turns out, you can! This picture is called a spectrogram, and it's a secret trick AI uses to 'see' sound instead of just 'hearing' it.

cards.title: What Is a Spectrogram?
cards.body: A spectrogram is a picture of sound. It shows time going across (left to right) and pitch/frequency going up (low to high). The colors or brightness show how loud each pitch is at each moment. It turns audio into an image!

cards.title: Like a Piano Roll
cards.body: Imagine a player piano roll with holes showing which notes play and when. A spectrogram is similar — instead of just one note, it shows ALL the pitches happening at every moment, like a super-detailed music map.

cards.title: Turning a Song Into an Image
cards.body: Say you have a 3-second clarinet note. A spectrogram turns it into an image where you see a bright horizontal stripe (the steady pitch) over those 3 seconds. AI can now use image tools — like the ones that generate pictures — to study, edit, or even generate new versions of this 'sound picture,' then turn it back into audio.

cards.title: Myth: AI Must 'Listen' Like Humans
cards.body: Myth: AI understands audio the same way our ears do. Truth: Many AI systems never 'hear' anything — they convert sound into a spectrogram image and use image-recognition or image-generation tricks on it. The 'listening' is really 'looking'!

cards.title: Sound Wave vs. Spectrogram
cards.body: Two different ways to show the exact same audio clip.

left.text: Shows loudness over time as one wiggly line — hard to see which pitches are present.

right.text: Shows time, pitch, and loudness all at once as a colorful image — easy for AI to analyze like a picture.

exercises.prompt: What does a spectrogram mainly show?

options.text: Frequency (pitch) over time as an image
The file size of an audio clip
The color of the microphone used

exercises.explain: A spectrogram plots time on one axis and frequency (pitch) on the other, with brightness/color showing loudness — turning sound into a picture.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Once audio is turned into a spectrogram, AI can use image-generation techniques on it.
exercises.explain: True! Since a spectrogram is basically a picture, AI models built for generating or editing images can be applied directly to it.

exercises.sentence: A spectrogram turns audio into a 2D ___ showing frequency over time.
exercises.explain: A spectrogram is a 2D image-like representation, which is why image-generation AI techniques can work on it.

exercises.prompt: Why do AI researchers convert audio into spectrograms before using generation models?

options.text: So they can reuse powerful image-generation techniques on the sound data
Because spectrograms make audio files smaller in storage size
Because computers cannot store audio in any other form

exercises.explain: Turning audio into a spectrogram lets researchers apply proven image-generation methods (like the ones used for pictures) directly to sound — no need to invent brand-new audio-only techniques.

exercises.prompt: An AI company wants to generate realistic bird chirp sounds using an image-generation model they already built for pictures. What's the smartest first step?

options.text: Convert bird sound recordings into spectrograms so the image model can work with them
Feed the raw audio wave files directly into the image model
Describe the bird sounds in a text file instead
Record the sounds as video instead of audio

exercises.explain: Since image-generation models work on 2D images, the smart move is to turn the audio into spectrograms first — now it's an image the model can understand and generate, and it can be converted back to sound afterward.
